reasoning: Using the FRED statistical trend analysis as primary anchor (30% probability). Economic context slightly increases this: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack's hawkish stance on rates ('should not have cut in October', questioning December cuts) and persistent inflation concerns provide upward pressure on real rates. However, recent downward momentum in the series and market expectations for lower policy rates support the statistical model's bearish view. February has higher uncertainty due to longer horizon and potential policy shifts, hence probability closer to 50% than December.
